channel.title: Hermann Park Events
channel.description: Upcoming events for Hermann Park Events

item.title: Moonlight Movie Nights: A Bug's Life
item.description:
Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: The Commons

As part of this summer’s Kicking It at Hermann Park offerings, enjoy “Moonlight Movie Nights,” presented by Goya, in the Commons! On Saturday, June 20, join us for a showing of A Bug’s Life (1998 | G | 1h 35m runtime). The movie will start at approximately 8:30 p.m., around sunset. The movie showing is free and open to the public. Be sure to get to the Commons early to snag your spot on the lawn. Guests are welcome to bring blankets or lawn chairs to settle in for the movie. Food and beverage will be available for purchase at the cafe and with food vendors on-site. Thank you to Goya for their generous support!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Pilates in the Park
item.description:
Sunday, June 21, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Molly Ann Smith Plaza, Hermann Park, Houston, TX, 77004, United States

Join us on Sunday, June 21, for free outdoor mat Pilates led by Revival Pilates. This evening class begins at 6 p.m. at Molly Ann Smith Plaza, adjacent to McGovern Lake and the Pioneer Memorial Obelisk. Be sure to bring a mat and water. You must sign up to attend! Book your spot HERE.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Yoga in the Park
item.description:
Sunday, June 21, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Molly Ann Smith Plaza, Hermann Park, Houston, TX, 77004, United States

Join us on Sunday, June 21, for morning yoga in the Commons led by Black Swan Yoga. This free evening session begins at 7 p.m. at Molly Ann Smith Plaza, adjacent to McGovern Lake and the Pioneer Memorial Obelisk. Be sure to bring a mat and water. You must sign up to attend! Book your spot HERE.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Wellness Walk
item.description:
Tuesday, June 23, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: TX

Join Laura Conely of Urban Paths for a fun evening as we explore the trails and beauty of Hermann Park on a guided 3-mile experience. All are invited to this conversationally paced walk; friendly pups on leash are welcome too! This event is free, but registration is required and space is limited. Sign up HERE. Participants will meet outside the McGovern Centennial Gardens, located at 1500 Hermann Drive, with parking available in adjacent Lot C. Laura Conely is the founder of Urban Paths, a walking wellness company. As a certified teacher and coach, she leads guided Wellness Walks that bring individuals of all ages and fitness levels together to experience the profound health benefits of: Nature, Art, Movement, and Connection.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, June 24, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Japanese Garden Volunteer Day
item.description:
Thursday, June 25, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Japanese Garden, Hermann Park Japanese Garden, Houston, TX, 77035, United States

Join us every Thursday for hands-on gardening projects at the Japanese Garden. From 9 a.m. to 12 p.m., come help with seasonal tasks like planting, weeding, mulching, and pruning. A great way to connect with others and make a visible impact in the Park! No experience needed. All ages are welcome. Click here to sign up!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Moonlight Movie Nights: Lilo & Stitch
item.description:
Saturday, June 27, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: The Commons

As part of this summer’s Kicking It at Hermann Park offerings, enjoy “Moonlight Movie Nights,” presented by Goya, in the Commons! On Saturday, June 27, join us for a showing of Lilo & Stitch (2002 | PG | 1h 25m runtime). The movie will start at approximately 8:30 p.m., around sunset. The movie showing is free and open to the public. Be sure to get to the Commons early to snag your spot on the lawn. Guests are welcome to bring blankets or lawn chairs to settle in for the movie. Food and beverage will be available for purchase at the cafe and with food vendors on-site. Thank you to Goya for their generous support!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.description:
Thursday, July 2, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Japanese Garden, Hermann Park Japanese Garden, Houston, TX, 77035, United States

Join us every Thursday for hands-on gardening projects at the Japanese Garden. From 9 a.m. to 12 p.m., come help with seasonal tasks like planting, weeding, mulching, and pruning. A great way to connect with others and make a visible impact in the Park! No experience needed. All ages are welcome. Click here to sign up!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Moonlight Movie Nights: National Treasure
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: The Commons

As part of this summer’s Kicking It at Hermann Park offerings, enjoy “Moonlight Movie Nights,” presented by Goya, in the Commons! On Friday, July 3, join us for a showing of National Treasure (2004 | PG | 2h 10m runtime). The movie will start at approximately 8:30 p.m., around sunset. The movie showing is free and open to the public. Be sure to get to the Commons early to snag your spot on the lawn. Guests are welcome to bring blankets or lawn chairs to settle in for the movie. Food and beverage will be available for purchase at the cafe and with food vendors on-site. Thank you to Goya for their generous support!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Red, White & Bloom
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: McGovern Centennial Gardens, 1500 Hermann Drive, Houston, TX, 77004, United States

Celebrate the Fourth of July surrounded by beautiful blooms, festive fun, and one of Houston’s most beloved fireworks displays! Join us for “Red, White & Bloom” on Saturday, July 4, from 8 – 11 p.m. in the McGovern Centennial Gardens for a fantastic summer evening. This year is extra special as we mark America’s 250th birthday with a celebration packed with birthday party vibes! Enjoy free treats, family-friendly activities, music, lawn games, and more before the night wraps up with a spectacular view of the fireworks show from Miller Outdoor Theatre. Best of all, Red, White & Bloom is FREE and open to the public! Bring a blanket, gather your friends and family, and stroll through the vibrant garden spaces before settling in for the show. Spend Independence Day in this iconic spot, under the stars—a perfect setting for summer memories! The fireworks show at Miller Outdoor Theatre is set to start at approximately 9:45 p.m. The complimentary food items are while supplies last. Additional food vendors will be offering items for purchase by Miller Outdoor Theatre.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Red, White & Cruise
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: Lake Plaza

Join us for Red, White & Cruise where you can watch Miller Outdoor Theatre’s fireworks show light up the sky from the peaceful waters of Hermann Park, with a special pedal boat rental from 8:30–10:30 p.m. on Saturday, July 4! As we celebrate 250 years of American independence, gather your crew and watch the sky come alive with fireworks while cruising across the lake in your own pedal boat under the stars. Saturday, July 4 8:30 – 10:30 p.m.  🎟️ $150 per boat (up to 4 people) includes: 🕘 Boat rental up to 2 hours 🍿 Drinks & snacks 🎊 Festive accessories 🅿️ Reserved parking in Lot I near Lake Plaza ⛵ Board anytime between 8:30–9:30 p.m. and enjoy the show from the water (fireworks begin at approximately 9:45 p.m., boats must return once fireworks finish) BOOK YOUR BOAT HERE!  🚨 Important Info: One rider per boat must be 18+ All riders 18+ must sign a digital waiver before boarding At least one rider must be 18+, with a physical government ID, and sign a waiver before boarding Tickets are non-refundable Event is rain or shine per Miller Outdoor Theatre’s plan In the event of lightning, pedal boats will close, and refunds will be issued  The purchase of a ticket includes an up-to 2-hour pedal boat ride for up to 4 people to enjoy the sight of Miller Outdoor Theatre’s fireworks show, drinks, snacks, festive treats, and a reserved parking spot in Lot I near Lott Hall by Lake Plaza (starting at 8 p.m.). Only one ticket purchase of $150 is needed for a 4-person boat (i.e. it is the cost per boat, not per rider).  A reminder that at least one rider per boat must be 18 years or older.  All riders 18 years or older must sign a digital waiver before boarding. Tickets are non-refundable. We are following Miller Outdoor Theatre’s plan for their event and fireworks show which is rain or shine. If there is lightning in the area, we will close the pedal boats and ticket holders will receive a refund.  Please arrive early to allow time to park in Lot I as roads in the Park will be closed by HPD and closures are subject to change based on their purview.  

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876.
